data.title: "A Blockbuster? Yes, Absolutely" — GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) Stock Slides despite Morez Hype
data.description:
Shares in GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) fell marginally early Monday even after the British drugmaker trumpeted the commercial potential of Morez, its experimental cancer drug.

"Do we think it would be ​a blockbuster? Yes, absolutely," Hesham Abdullah, GSK's senior vice president and global head of oncology research and development, told journalists on Sunday in a call.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market News, 4/13/26 – Futures Sink as Trump Orders Hormuz Blockade, Oil Rockets Past $100
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Monday morning as global markets reacted to President Trump's order to block the Strait of Hormuz. The move came after weekend peace talks in Islamabad failed, raising fears of a longer conflict and a sharp rise in energy prices. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.61%, 0.51%, and 0.57%, respectively, at 5:30 a.m. EDT on April 13.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

In a post on Truth Social, Trump said the U.S. Navy would begin blocking ships entering or leaving the Strait of Hormuz. He added that other countries would join the effort and accused Iran of using the route for "illegal extortion."

The collapse of talks in Islamabad has increased fears that tensions between the U.S. and Iran could drag on. This has already pushed oil prices sharply higher, adding pressure on the global economy. The international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) rose 8.03% to $102.83 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) gained 8.16% to $104.45 per barrel.

Wall Street had just wrapped up a strong week, as optimism around a temporary ceasefire lifted markets and helped all three major indexes post solid gains, with the S&P 500 up 3.6%, the Nasdaq rising about 4.7%, and the Dow gaining 3%.

Now, investor focus is also turning to earnings. The first-quarter reporting season begins this week, led by major banks. Goldman Sachs (GS) is set to report on Monday, followed by Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Morgan Stanley (MS), and Bank of America (BAC) later in the week.

Elsewhere, major European indexes traded lower as ceasefire hopes faded.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed despite reports of a U.S. naval blockade on Iran's ports followed failed talks between Washington and Tehran.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index dropped 0.90%. At the same time, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.74% and the Topix fell 0.45%. However, in China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.059% and the Shenzhen Component was up 0.69%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Palo Alto (PANW), CrowdStrike (CRWD), and Akamai (AKAM) Slide after Anthropic Sparks New Cyber Fears
data.description:
U.S. cybersecurity stocks have come under pressure in recent days as investors react to new risks tied to advanced AI tools. The shift follows an announcement from Anthropic about its latest model, Claude Mythos Preview, which has raised fresh concerns about how fast cyber threats could evolve.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade AMZN with leverage

Over a three-day period, several major names saw sharp declines. Palo Alto Networks (PANW) fell about 12%, while Akamai Technologies (AKAM) dropped nearly 17% on Friday, and 20% overall. CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD), SentinelOne (S), and Fortinet (FTNT) also moved lower, with losses near 11% and 8%, respectively. The move reflects a broader concern that AI may change how cyber risks are found and used.

At the center of the issue is the model itself. Anthropic said Mythos is highly effective at finding software flaws, including some that have existed for many years. In some cases, the model was able to detect bugs that had gone unnoticed for decades. Because of this, the company chose not to release it to the public. Instead, access is limited to a small group of firms that manage key systems.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, April 12
data.description:
Last week was marked by rising indexes; however, it ended on a mixed note, with stocks holding firm while oil and inflation remained in focus. The Dow Jones (DJIA) fell 0.56% to 47,916, while the S&P 500 (SPX) slipped 0.11% and the Nasdaq (NDX) rose 0.14%. At the same time, the U.S. 10-year yield moved up to 4.32%, and oil prices (CM:CL) held near $95.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

This setup reflects a market that is steady on the surface but still shaped by energy risk and rate pressure.

Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) traded near $73,000, and gold (CM:XAUUSD) held close to record levels, suggesting that some investors are still seeking hedges.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Oil Hits $100+ as U.S. Threatens Blockade on Iran — Which Stocks Are in the Spotlight?
data.description:
Oil prices jumped above $100 per barrel on Monday after President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to block ships from Iranian ports. This came after talks to end the ongoing U.S.-Israel-Iran war hit a wall, putting last week's two-week ceasefire on shaky ground.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

TipRanks Best Oil Stocks tool shows that analysts believe crude oil producers such as ConocoPhillips (COP) and Chevron (CVX) stand to attract the biggest upside as the Middle East conflict keeps energy prices elevated.

As of 4:12 a.m. EST, the global oil futures benchmark Brent Crude (CM:BZ) was up 7.70% to $103.95, while the key U.S. oil gauge, the West Texas Intermediate (CM:CL), jumped by 8.59% to $103.98.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: M&A News: Baker Hughes (BKR) Stock Rises on $1.45B Waygate Sale to Hexagon
data.description:
Shares of Baker Hughes (BKR) are rising in pre-market trading on Monday after the company announced a major asset sale. The company has agreed to sell its Waygate Technologies unit to Sweden-based Hexagon (HXGBY) for about $1.45 billion in cash. Through this and other recent deals, Baker Hughes is streamlining its business to boost cash flow, strengthen its balance sheet, and support long-term growth. As of this writing, BKR stock is up about 0.43% in pre-market trading.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

For context, Baker Hughes is an energy technology company that provides equipment and services to oil, gas, and industrial sectors worldwide. Meanwhile, Hexagon is a global industrial technology firm that provides digital solutions for measurement, imaging, and data analytics across multiple industries.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Hims & Hers (HIMS) Shifts Beyond GLP-1 Controversy to Long-Term Health Platform
data.description:
Hims & Hers (HIMS) is moving from the controversy surrounding glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) to a long-term health platform. Not that long ago, it looked like a vulnerable growth name with a legal cloud over it. Now the picture is different. The business looks more credible, more connected, and better positioned for the long run.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

We have moved from debating how Hims & Hers fits around compounded weight-loss drugs to whether it can turn its digital reach into a durable position across branded treatments and the emerging demand for longevity. That is why, despite its near-term market volatility, I remain bullish on HIMS stock.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: CarMax Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Options Trades Expect a Major Move in KMX Stock
data.description:
Used-car retailer CarMax (KMX) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2026 before the market opens on Tuesday, April 14. KMX stock has risen 21% year-to-date, driven by optimism about the company's turnaround efforts and improving demand amid geopolitical tensions and macro uncertainty. Last week, CarMax announced the appointment of two independent directors and reached a settlement with activist investor Starboard.

According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 10.53% move in either direction in KMX stock in reaction to Q4 FY26 earnings. This implied move is higher than CarMax stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 5.72% over the past four quarters.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: McDonald's (MCD) Stock Looks Stuck — Can Its Energy Drink Push Finally Move It?
data.description:
McDonald's (MCD) may be making a small change that could have a bigger impact on its U.S. business. According to the Wall Street Journal, the fast-food giant is adding energy drinks and specialty sodas to its menu, including options like a Red Bull-based beverage, as it looks to expand into higher-margin beverage sales. MCD stock hasn't done much lately, especially as investors have focused on AI and other fast-growing sectors. But this move into drinks could help drive more customer traffic and improve margins over time, something long-term investors may not be fully pricing in yet.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

For context, McDonald's is a global fast-food company known for its burgers, fries, and growing focus on high-margin beverages and franchise-driven profits. Year-to-date, MCD stock is basically flat, up just 0.02%.
data.author: Kirti Tak
